response.justification: The candidate is a strong technical match on paper — deep TypeScript/Node.js fullstack experience, React exposure, Kubernetes and AWS knowledge, and recent hands-on LLM/MCP integration work that covers most nice-to-haves. However, with roughly 15+ years of experience and staff/senior-level titles, he is significantly overqualified for an entry-level junior/mid position, raising real questions about salary alignment, engagement with junior-scoped work, and retention. If the company is open to leveling flexibility or the candidate has explicit reasons for seeking this role, a conversation is worthwhile.
strengths: Extensive TypeScript/Node.js fullstack experience across product companies
Recent LLM, MCP, and AI-agent tooling experience matching nice-to-have criteria
DevOps depth: Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, CI/CD, and TDD craftsmanship
concerns: Heavily overqualified for an entry-level role; retention and salary-fit risk
No explicit PostgreSQL or Nest.js experience (MongoDB/MySQL listed instead)
Current career gap and unclear motivation for stepping down in seniority
